history.text: In more recent history, the Tlokwa left the Magaliesberg ranges to settle in the area around 1880, and called the settlement Moshaweng.
In 1890, Cecil John Rhodes picked Gaberones to house a colonial fort.

The modern town was only founded in 1964, after a decision was taken to establish a capital for Botswana, which became a self governing territory in 1965, before becoming a fully independent republic on.
In 1965, the capital of the Bechuanaland Protectorate moved from Mafeking to Gaberones.
On 30 September 1966, Bechuanaland became the eleventh British dependency in Africa to become independent.
The city changed its name from Gaberones to Gaborone in 1969.

In 1971, because of the growth of illegal settlements, the Gaborone Town Council and the Ministry of Local Government and Lands surveyed an area called Bontleng, which would contain low-income housing.
In 1973, the Botswana Housing Corporation (BHC) built a "New Naledi" across the road from the "Old Naledi".

history.label: Civic role
history.text: Gaberones, a shortening of "Gaborone's Village", was named after Chief Gaborone of the Tlokwa, whose home village (now called Tlokweng) was across the river from the Government Camp, the name of the colonial government.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: When Botswana gained its independence, Lobatse was the first choice as the nation's capital.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: However, Lobatse was deemed too limited, and instead, a new capital city would be created next to Gaberones.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The city was chosen because of its proximity to a fresh water source, its proximity to the railway to Pretoria, its central location among the central tribes, and its lack of association with those.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Because the town was built so quickly, there was a massive influx of labourers who had built illegal settlements on the new city's southern industrial development zone.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: This has led to many squatter settlements on undeveloped land.
